factors.label: Website content relevance and completeness
factors.instruction: Score only how well the website's own content proves relevance and completeness for the query. Include service/product fit, features, product materials, menus, service range, compliance, coverage details, pricing pages, booking paths, and category-specific information here.

factors.label: Reviews and customer reputation
factors.instruction: Score only customer review and reputation signals. Consider review volume, review quality, recency, rating consistency, customer sentiment, complaints, and platform credibility. Do not score website content here except as evidence of reviews/testimonials.

factors.label: Third-party authority and external validation
factors.instruction: Score only independent external validation. Consider editorial lists, awards, government or industry registers, reputable directories, marketplace rankings, expert reviews, forum consensus, and credible media mentions.

factors.label: On-site trust signals
factors.instruction: Score only trust signals visible on the website itself. Consider clear contact details, ABN/company details, security/privacy pages, guarantees, insurance, certifications, policies, case studies, testimonials, client logos, team/about pages, and professional presentation.

factors.label: Location, availability, and service coverage
factors.instruction: Score only how well the website/provider fits the user's geography and availability needs. For local services, consider Sydney service area, proximity, booking availability, opening hours, and delivery/service logistics. For national products/software, consider Australian availability and local support.

factors.label: Price and value clarity
factors.instruction: Score only pricing and value clarity. Consider visible pricing, plan/product comparison, inclusions, value for money, free trials, quotes, shipping/extra fees, and whether a buyer can judge affordability.

trust_audit.summary: For an Australian business comparing visitor management vendors, the most useful trust sources are B2B software review platforms for product fit, Google Business/Profile and local review ecosystems for implementation/service reputation, official vendor websites for feature verification, and high-signal industry/editorial sources for market positioning. In this category, G2 and Capterra are especially relevant because the products are software-first and have visible review coverage for Envoy, Vizito, and Gallagher. Trustpilot is relevant for vendor reputation checks, but its utility is usually lower than G2/Capterra for B2B product selection. Reddit is useful only as a low-confidence sentiment source, not as a primary trust source. Official sites and product pages are the strongest source for confirming whether a vendor actually offers visitor management, while source-security/editorial PDFs help validate enterprise/security positioning.

trust_sources.reason_ai_trusted_source: Capterra is highly relevant for B2B software comparison because it hosts product-specific user reviews and category pages for visitor management software, making it useful for evaluating product fit and implementation experience. It also had visible review pages for Envoy and Vizito in search results.

G2 is a major software review platform with product-level review pages and category pages. For visitor management evaluation, it is especially useful when a vendor has enough reviews to show patterns in usability, support, and deployment. Search results showed active review pages for Envoy, Vizito, and Gallagher.

Trustpilot is relevant for broad reputation checking because it captures public customer sentiment and is easy to find for vendor-name searches. It is less specialized for B2B software than G2/Capterra, so it should be used as a secondary signal rather than the main decision source.

Google reviews are important for Australian buyers because they are widely used, location-aware, and often reflect service quality, onboarding, and local support experience. They matter most when vendors have Australian offices, resellers, installers, or support teams.

ProductReview matters specifically for Australian purchasing decisions because it is locally oriented and can capture buyer experience, delivery, and support issues that global SaaS review sites may miss. It is most useful when an Australian vendor or local implementation partner has an active presence there.

Reddit is useful for qualitative sentiment, peer recommendations, and pain-point discovery, but it is not a high-confidence source because posts are anecdotal and unverifiable. It can still be helpful for spotting vendor mentions and framing follow-up questions.

Official vendor websites are the primary source for confirming that a company actually sells visitor management software and for verifying product features, integrations, compliance claims, deployment models, and supported industries. For this category, official sites are essential because many vendors have similar naming and positioning but different feature depth.

Industry/editorial sources are useful for enterprise/security-focused visitor management because they provide product datasheets, positioning, and deployment context that help validate whether a solution is enterprise-grade and security-oriented.

No specific government register or authoritative industry register was verified in the web search results for these vendors, but this source type would matter for checking company registration, certifications, and compliance claims in an Australian procurement process.

No marketplace evidence was verified in the search results. This source type would matter if the buyer were checking app marketplaces or procurement marketplaces for integrations, listing age, and ecosystem traction.
Tripadvisor was considered because it is a major review platform, but it is generally not relevant for B2B visitor management software and did not surface as a meaningful source in the searches.

Industry directories matter when verifying vendor presence, category fit, and partner ecosystem, but no specific directory source was clearly verified in the search results beyond software review directories like G2 and Capterra.
Social platforms can matter for awareness and informal peer validation, but no specific platform evidence was verified in the search results for these vendors.
